name: Tranqueru Greenway
name_original: Perlora, Vía Verde del Tranqueru, Desembocadura Ría de Aboño
description: Hike the moderate 5.8-mile Perlora, Vía Verde del Tranqueru, Mouth of the Aboño Estuary trail for coastal views and railway history.
region_intro: Hiking trails around Carreño, located in Spain, offer a variety of outdoor experiences. The region is characterized by its dramatic coastal views, notably around Cabo Peñas, and features diverse natural monuments. Hikers can explore a wide array of routes that traverse these varied landscapes. The terrain includes coastal paths and areas with moderate elevation changes. Best hiking trails around Carreño The most popular hiking route is Tranqueru Greenway, a 5.8 miles (9.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a greenway, offering accessible paths. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of Perlora – Candás loop from Xivares, a moderate 8.1 miles (13.0 km) path. This loop provides expansive views of the coastline and surrounding areas. Local hikers also love the Perlora Holiday Town – Candás loop from Candás Apeaderu, a 5.9 miles (9.6 km) trail leading through coastal towns and natural areas, often completed in about 2 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around Carreño is defined by coastal paths, natural monuments, and varied terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging paths. The routes in Carreño are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from nearly 200 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Carreño's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Carreño?
A: Carreño offers a diverse network of over 25 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These routes explore the region's dramatic coastal views and natural monuments.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hiking trails in Carreño?
A: The trails in Carreño range from easy to difficult. You'll find approximately 12 easy routes, 11 moderate paths, and 2 more challenging trails, ensuring options for all hikers.

Q: Are there any easy or short hikes suitable for beginners in Carreño?
A: Yes, Carreño has several easy and short routes perfect for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048450510/tranqueru-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranqueru Greenway&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.8-mile (9.4 km) trail that follows an accessible greenway, ideal for a relaxed walk.

Q: Can I find family-friendly hiking routes in Carreño?
A: Many of Carreño's easier trails are suitable for families. Look for routes with lower elevation gain and shorter distances, such as the greenways or coastal paths that offer gentle terrain and scenic views without being overly strenuous.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Carreño?
A: While specific regulations can vary by trail, generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in Carreño, especially if kept on a leash. Always ensure you clean up after your pet to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Carreño?
A: Yes, Carreño features several popular circular routes. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20507741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Perlora – Candás loop from Xivares&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.1-mile (13.0 km) moderate loop offering expansive coastal views. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17682568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Perlora Holiday Town – Candás loop from Candás Apeaderu&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.9 miles (9.6 km) and passes through coastal towns.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Carreño?
A: Carreño is rich in natural beauty and landmarks. You can explore the dramatic cliffs and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4016988" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo Peñas Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, which stands 100 meters above sea level. The region also boasts various natural monuments and stunning viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5575818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Cervigón&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/473306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Providencia Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any coastal walks with great views in Carreño?
A: Absolutely! Carreño is renowned for its stunning coastal paths. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15101331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Rebolleres – Playa de El Dique, L'Aramar loop from Candás Apeaderu&lt;/a&gt; offer breathtaking sea views and opportunities to explore beautiful beaches.

Q: How can I get to the trailheads, and is parking available?
A: Many trailheads in Carreño are accessible by car, and parking is generally available in nearby towns or designated areas. For some routes, public transport options like local buses or train stops (e.g., Candás Apeaderu) can also provide access. It's advisable to check specific route details for the best access points.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Carreño?
A: The best time to hike in Carreño is typically during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and pleasant, and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer also offers good conditions, especially for coastal walks, though it can be busier. Winters are generally mild but can be wet.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the towns and villages around Carreño, such as Candás and Perlora, offer various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Many trails pass through or near these settlements, making it convenient to find refreshments or a place to stay.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Carreño?
A: The hiking trails in Carreño are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from nearly 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse coastal scenery, well-maintained paths, and the variety of routes suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by historical or cultural sites?
A: Yes, some trails in Carreño offer glimpses into the region's history and culture. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31887195" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Eulalia Church – Traditional Hórreo loop from Carreño&lt;/a&gt; takes you past traditional Asturian architecture. You can also find historical monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4392222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Mother of the Emigrant&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4885149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elogio del Horizonte&lt;/a&gt; in the wider area.
tips: The area is popular during the summer months due to its beautiful beach. In addition, its Maritime Research Center and its Maritime Museum are places of interest.
Nice beach with a special charm. At high tide, you only have a piece of beach with stones. It is accessed by a route of little difficulty.
A wonderful and open beach (at low tide). It connects with the north path to Candas. There is a picnic area to rest for a while.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Vía Verde del Tranqueru?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily follows the route of an old railway line, so it's relatively flat. The path is composed of compacted earth and gravel, making it suitable for various activities. You'll encounter a mix of natural coastal landscapes and areas near industrial zones.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and landmarks can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect stunning views of the Cantabrian Sea and dramatic cliffs. The route features old railway tunnels and bridges, remnants of its past. You'll also see beautiful beaches like Tranqueru Beach and Playa de Xivares. Near Perlora Holiday Town, you can observe the 'ghost town' of the Ciudad Residencial de Perlora.

faqs.question: Is the Vía Verde del Tranqueru accessible for people with reduced mobility?
faqs.answer: Yes, due to its relatively flat path of compacted earth and gravel, the Vía Verde del Tranqueru is generally considered accessible for people with reduced mobility.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily linear, following the former railway line. You'll typically hike it as an out-and-back route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn often offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds. Summer is also popular, especially for enjoying the coastal views and beaches.

faqs.question: Is the Perlora, Vía Verde del Tranqueru, Mouth of the Aboño Estuary trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Vía Verde del Tranqueru is generally dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is under control, and be mindful of other users and local regulations regarding leashes, especially near beaches or populated areas.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Vía Verde del Tranqueru?
faqs.answer: Parking is available at various points along the route. For specific trailhead parking, it's advisable to check local maps or online resources for designated parking areas near Perlora or Xivares.

faqs.question: How can I get to the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: Public transport options may be available to nearby towns like Perlora or Xivares, from which you can access the Vía Verde. It's best to consult local bus or train schedules for the most current information.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and hike the Vía Verde del Tranqueru.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect to other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is a segment of the broader Coastal Path E-9. It also overlaps with sections of the official trails named Vía Verde del Tranqueru, Senda Norte, and Acceso Playa.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities near the Vía Verde del Tranqueru path?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is natural, you can find cafes and other facilities in the nearby towns and settlements such as Perlora and Xivares, which are easily accessible from the Vía Verde.
